description: Typed JSONL event log schema + append helper + reader helpers for history/memory.jsonl — orchestrator-owned, agents never touch it (ADR-0001).

userStories.title: MemoryEvent type + EventType enum
userStories.description: As the orchestrator, I want a TypeScript type for memory events so that every row written to history/memory.jsonl is structurally validated at compile time.
acceptanceCriteria: File scripts/memory.ts exists with first line: // ORCHESTRATOR-ONLY: agents must not import this module directly (ADR-0001)
EventType is a string union of exactly 6 values: promote | rollback | skip | regression | gap-detected | verdict-ready
EventRefs is typed as Record<string, string>
MemoryEvent interface has fields: ts (string), week (string), actor (string), event (EventType), refs (EventRefs), insight (string) — no optional fields, no extra fields
MemoryFilter type is Partial<Pick<MemoryEvent, 'week' | 'actor' | 'event'>>
All types are exported named exports
No `any` without a justification comment
bun run type-check passes
userStories.technicalNotes: Create scripts/memory.ts. Type naming: CapitalCase per scripts/critic-genealogy.ts:31-68. JSON-compat keys use snake_case (e.g. refs.exp_id). No default exports. No src/ directory — scripts/ is the right location.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration 1. Files: scripts/memory.ts. Commit: 3670941.

userStories.title: appendEvent helper with idempotency
userStories.description: As the orchestrator, I want to append a single MemoryEvent to history/memory.jsonl so that every significant action is durably recorded, with deduplication when an exp_id is present.
acceptanceCriteria: appendEvent(event: MemoryEvent, logPath?: string): void — logPath defaults to 'history/memory.jsonl' relative to process.cwd()
Creates history/ directory with mkdirSync({ recursive: true }) if it doesn't exist
Creates memory.jsonl if it doesn't exist on first call
Appends exactly one JSON-encoded line (JSON.stringify(event) + newline) per non-duplicate call
Idempotency: if refs.exp_id is present AND a row with identical (ts, week, actor, event, refs.exp_id) already exists, the function returns without writing
If refs.exp_id is absent, always appends (no dedup check)
fs errors propagate — no try/catch swallowing errors
bun run type-check passes
userStories.technicalNotes: Use appendFileSync from node:fs for atomic line appends. mkdirSync pattern from scripts/critic-genealogy.ts:573-590. For idempotency: readFileSync the existing file (if it exists), parse lines, check for matching tuple before appending. Dedup check only when refs.exp_id is truthy.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration 2. Files: scripts/memory.ts, context/FEATURES.md, context/DOMAIN-MODEL.md, context/ROADMAP.md, context/v2-design.md, context/critics/*/findings.md, .forge/ralph/feat-memory-substrate/prd.md, .forge/ralph/feat-memory-substrate/progress.txt. Commit: 5d16fa9.

userStories.title: tailN + filter reader helpers
userStories.description: As the orchestrator, I want to read recent or filtered events from history/memory.jsonl so that the planner can be given a curated history slice without re-reading all raw artifacts.
acceptanceCriteria: tailN(n: number, logPath?: string): MemoryEvent[] — returns the last n events from the log; returns [] if file doesn't exist
filter(criteria: MemoryFilter, logPath?: string): MemoryEvent[] — returns all events matching ALL provided criteria fields; returns [] if file doesn't exist
Both functions skip malformed lines (non-JSON) and throw an error identifying the bad line number
tailN(0) returns []
tailN(n) where n > total events returns all events
filter({}) with empty criteria returns all events
Both functions return typed MemoryEvent[] — no unknown or any return types
bun run type-check passes
userStories.technicalNotes: Read file with readFileSync, split on newline, filter empty lines, JSON.parse each. Tail: slice from end. Filter: check each criteria key for equality. Both accept optional logPath defaulting to 'history/memory.jsonl'. Pattern: readFileSync from scripts/critic-genealogy.ts:162.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration 3. Files: scripts/memory.ts. Commit: b6ca55b.

userStories.title: Unit tests: round-trip, idempotency, tailN, filter
userStories.description: As a developer, I want unit tests for all memory substrate helpers so that regressions are caught before merge.
acceptanceCriteria: Test file at scripts/__tests__/memory.test.ts using bun:test (describe, test, expect imports)
Schema round-trip test: construct a MemoryEvent with all 6 event values, JSON.stringify + JSON.parse, assert equality
appendEvent test: write 3 events to a temp file, read back with tailN(3), assert all 3 present
Idempotency test: append same event (with refs.exp_id) twice, assert file contains exactly 1 line
No-dedup test: append same event WITHOUT refs.exp_id twice, assert file contains exactly 2 lines
tailN test: write 5 events, tailN(3) returns last 3 in order
filter test: write events with 2 different actors, filter({ actor: 'planner' }) returns only planner events
filter empty criteria test: filter({}) returns all events
Temp files use a unique path per test (e.g. /tmp/memory-test-{Date.now()}.jsonl) to avoid cross-test contamination
bun test passes with zero failures
bun run validate passes
userStories.technicalNotes: Test framework: bun:test — import { describe, test, expect } from 'bun:test'. Pattern from scripts/__tests__/critic-genealogy.test.ts:44-76. Use Date.now() or crypto.randomUUID() for unique temp file paths. Clean up temp files in each test or accept they're ephemeral in /tmp.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration 4. Files: scripts/__tests__/memory.test.ts, context/FEATURES.md, .forge/ralph/feat-memory-substrate/prd.json, .forge/ralph/feat-memory-substrate/progress.txt. Commit: ca357d4.
